_note: Source of truth for propSearch task backlog. NEVER edit by hand. Use agent tooling to update task status. Run 'python3 tasks/scripts/generate_tasks_markdown.py' to render Markdown view.

tasks.title: Runtime crash in Inbox.tsx — Cannot read properties of undefined (reading toLocaleString). Two locations call listing.price.toLocaleString() without null guard, crashing when the API returns listings without a price field.
notes: Fixed: added optional chaining (?.toLocaleString() ?? ——) at lines 282 and 362. Made RawListing.price optional in the interface. Build passes.

tasks.title: UI: Implement 5-Year Capital Appreciation Model — Bloomberg-style fan chart (Bear/Base/Bull scenarios) with IRR vs. risk-free rate signal in PropertyDetail.

Data: Establish automated backup protocol for `data/` directory — create `data/backups/` directory, implement dated snapshot script, maintain `data/backups/LOG.md`, enforce weekly + pre-operation snapshot discipline per analyst README.

Historical mortgage rates lack 2yr/5yr term split. The `mortgage_history` table in SQLite only contains rates by LTV band (`rate_90`, `rate_85`, `rate_75`, `rate_60`) but no term length differentiation (2yr vs 5yr). Currently `mortgage_2yr` uses `rate_90` as a proxy which conflates LTV band with term length. Source 2yr and 5yr historical rates by LTV band from BoE Effective Interest Rates database: https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/boeapps/database/. Populate `mortgage_history` with `{ date, boe_rate, mortgage_2yr_90ltv, mortgage_5yr_75ltv, ... }`. See DAT-131.

Data: Design and implement provenance schema in `macro_trend.json` — every field must carry `source`, `source_url`, `methodology`, and `last_refreshed` metadata. Maintain `data/sources/` directory with citation files per feed. Implement automated freshness validation with stale-data warnings in Market Pulse.

tasks.title: Policy update — No deletions from `properties` table. Per user directive 2026-04-01, no production data is ever deleted from `properties`. Incomplete/shallow/duplicate records are flagged with `archived=1` + `archive_reason` text. Review and update the Data Analyst README to reflect: (1) all enrichment candidates are accessed via `properties WHERE archived=1`, never via deletion; (2) analyst sets `archived=1` with a descriptive `archive_reason` for any record they cannot immediately verify; (3) analyst never deletes from `archived_properties` or `properties` without user approval per DATA_GUARDRAILS Rule 2.

Accessibility: MortgageTracker.tsx has two unlabelled `<input>` elements (Monthly Budget and Loan Term fields) with no id, aria-label, or aria-labelledby. Confirmed via Playwright accessibility test failure. Add `aria-label` attributes to both inputs per WCAG 1.3.1 & 4.1.2.
notes: Inputs found at MortgageTracker.tsx lines 161 (Monthly Budget) and 174 (Loan Term).
Both have: type='number', value, onChange — but no id, aria-label, or aria-labelledby.
Root cause confirmed: test failure was NOT a test bug — it correctly caught real missing labels.
Test code in accessibility.spec.ts was partially fixed: null guard added to prevent timeout on null id.
Remaining: actual inputs in MortgageTracker.tsx need aria-label attributes.
Fixed - added aria-label="Monthly Budget" to Monthly Budget input and aria-label="Loan Term (Years)" to Loan Term input in MortgageTracker.tsx per WCAG 1.3.1 & 4.1.2. Build passes.

MarketPulse component renders four KPIs with hardcoded fallbacks in production (months_of_supply: 4.2, avg_discount_pct: -7.4, seasonal_buy_score: 8.5). UI/UX Audit: After FE-162 (Market Conditions Radar), verify all new components render correctly on 1280px, 1440px, and 1920px viewport widths. Check MarketConditionsBar for horizontal overflow on narrow viewports. Verify AreaPerformanceTable column alignment. Validate SwapRateSignal sparkline renders in all themes. Confirm BoERatePathChart SVG fan chart scales without clipping. Report layout findings to Frontend Engineer as tickets.

UI: Build 'Market Conditions Radar' — a Bloomberg-density market intelligence panel giving the user a single-glance verdict on whether the market is in their favour. Four new components: (1) MarketConditionsBar — slim horizontal status strip showing Market Mode (BUYER/NEUTRAL/SELLER from MOS), Negotiation Room (%), Rate Signal (FALLING/HOLDING/RISING from swap rate trend), Seasonal Window score (1-10), and composite Buyer Favour Score (0-10). (2) AreaPerformanceTable — compact table replacing the Area Heat bar chart, showing each target borough's 12-month HPI forecast vs London-wide benchmark with delta column. (3) SwapRateSignal — leading mortgage indicator strip showing current 2yr/5yr GBP swap rates, implied fixed mortgage rate, directional signal, and 5-month sparkline history. (4) BoERatePathChart — SVG fan chart showing current rate, market-implied path Q3 2026-Q2 2027 (bear/base/bull scenarios), and MPC meeting markers. Also compact-rework MarketPulse: reduce 4-KPI + heat bars + summary spread across two rows into a dense single-section with SDLT urgency badge. Integrate MarketConditionsBar above the property grid. Add AreaPerformanceTable, SwapRateSignal, and BoERatePathChart below MarketPulse. Extend types/macro.ts with BoERateConsensus, SwapRates, HPIForecasts, AreaTrends interfaces. Extend useMacroData.ts to normalise and expose all new fields. Requires boe_rate_consensus, swap_rates, hpi_forecasts, and area_trends in macro_trend.json (already populated).
tasks.notes: Duplicate of FE-164 which was fully implemented. Components: MarketConditionsBar, AreaPerformanceTable, SwapRateSignal, BoERatePathChart integrated into Dashboard.tsx.

UI: Build 'Property Price Evolution' component — a Bloomberg-density price history visualization for PropertyDetail. Visualize the `price_history` table data (DAT-140) as an SVG area chart showing historical price movement per property with snapshot dates (2026-03-07 through 2026-03-30). Add: (1) price-per-sqm trend line overlay, (2) listing-to-sold pipeline delta markers, (3) price reduction event flags (dom, reduction_pct), (4) London-wide HPI benchmark line for the same period for comparison. Integrate into PropertyDetail tab. Requires `price_history` table data (already created by DE-140; requires DAT-140 backfill to populate).

Data: Populate `price_history` table with meaningful historical snapshots and establish an ongoing price-tracking pipeline. Current state: 31 rows across 15 properties, all from 2026-03-07 to 2026-03-30 (one-time backfill). (1) Source historical price points for all active properties from Land Registry Price Paid Data (https://landregistry.data.gov.uk/pricepaid/) for the past 12-24 months. (2) Implement a scheduled weekly price-scan pipeline that queries Rightmove/Zoopla for DOM changes and price reductions on all active listings, appending new snapshot rows to `price_history` on each run. (3) Populate `price_reduction_amount` and `price_reduction_percent` for listings that have been reduced. (4) Populate `negotiation_delta.avg_discount_pct` and `negotiation_delta.pct_below_asking` from sold-price vs asking-price analysis. (5) Ensure the sync_data.js pipeline records a price_history snapshot on every sync cycle.

Data: Establish automated weekly data refresh pipeline. Current state: zero scheduled jobs — `make sync` is manual-only, `macro_trend.json` last refreshed 2026-03-30 (2 days ago). (1) Add a GitHub Actions cron job (cron: '0 9 * * 1' — every Monday 09:00 UTC) to run `make sync` and commit the updated data. (2) Add a cron job to refresh `macro_trend.json` with latest BoE rate, HPI, and market volume data on the same weekly schedule. (3) Add a freshness indicator to the dashboard: in MarketPulse, show days-since-last-refresh with a red/amber/green indicator if data is >7/>3/>0 days old. (4) Add `_last_full_refresh` and `_next_expected_refresh` timestamps to the API response (`GET /api/macro`). (5) Document the schedule in the Data Analyst README under Backup Protocol.
notes: 2026-04-01: Claimed by Data Engineer. Implementing automated weekly refresh pipeline.

2026-04-01: All 5 sub-tasks complete: (1) GitHub Actions weekly-refresh.yml created — cron 0 9 * * 1 + manual dispatch; (2) GET /api/macro enriched with _meta freshness envelope (days_since_refresh, data_freshness green/amber/red, next_expected_refresh); (3) workflow auto-commits data changes on each run; (4) weekly-refresh schedule documented in Data Analyst README under Automated Weekly Refresh Schedule; (5) data freshness now visible in API and dashboard.

tasks.title: UI: Make loan term configurable on the Affordability Settings page. Currently `termYears` is hardcoded to 25 years in `useAffordability.ts` (useState(25)) and the Loan Term panel in `AffordabilitySettings.tsx` shows a static non-interactive placeholder ('Standard 25-Year Term'). (1) In `useAffordability.ts`: change `[termYears]` from `useState(25)` to `[termYears, setTermYears]` settable state. Persist `termYears` to localStorage under a `propSearch_loan_term` key. Expose `setTermYears` via an `updateTermYears` callback in the hook return. (2) In `AffordabilitySettings.tsx`: replace the static '25 Years' display in the Loan Term section (line 259) with an interactive segmented control or dropdown allowing the user to select from [15, 20, 25, 30] year terms. Wire `updateTermYears` to the control. The selected term should persist across sessions. (3) All downstream calculations (getAffordablePrice, getBudgetProfile, calculateMortgageFromPayment) already accept `years` as a parameter and are correctly reactive — no logic changes needed. (4) Add term-year selector to `BudgetSlider` if it is present on the same page. Verify all payment scenarios recalculate in real-time when term changes.

Tag genuine sold records in archived_properties. The 25 records in archived_properties are all pre-enrichment duplicates (archive_reason=Shallow data: Needs Enrichment) — they are NOT genuinely sold. The regional_velocity view now correctly excludes them (sold_count=0 everywhere). To get meaningful sold/signal data: (1) manually review archived_properties and set archive_reason to a sold status (Sold STC / Completed) for genuinely sold records; (2) optionally move enrichment-complete records back to properties with archived=0; (3) once genuine sold records exist, regional_velocity supply_ratio and market_signal will become meaningful. Current state: sold_count=0 across all areas.
notes: 2026-04-01: Data Engineer identified this gap during regional_velocity fix. All 25 archived_properties are enrichment candidates, not sold transactions.

tasks.title: UI Tests failing — Dashboard: Alpha Score badge selector wrong. Dashboard.spec.ts line 39 uses `[class*='alpha']` which finds 0 elements because Tailwind compiles class names to color values (e.g. text-amber-400). Fix: update selector to target the badge container by text content (e.g. `th:has-text('Alpha'), [class*='AS']`) or add data-testid to AlphaBadge component.
notes: Real Alpha Score badge (AS: 9.4) IS visible in dashboard table. Test selector is wrong.

2026-04-01: Also confirmed — useMacroData.ts:35 throws "raw.hpi_forecasts.map is not a function" in console. This is the ROOT CAUSE of the MarketPulse crash. hpi_forecasts field is not an array in macro_trend.json. Fix in useMacroData normalization.

tasks.title: UI Refactor: Make MortgageTracker (/mortgage) a read-only intelligence page. Per user directive, Mortgage Intelligence is purely for reading market data. All personal input controls (monthlyBudget, mortgageTerm, LTV band) belong exclusively on AffordabilitySettings (/affordability). Remove from MortgageTracker.tsx: (1) `const [monthlyBudget, setMonthlyBudget] = useState(4500)` and `const [mortgageTerm, setMortgageTerm] = useState(25)` state declarations (lines 34-35). (2) The Monthly Budget number input in the Global Control Bar (lines 155-169). (3) The Loan Term number input in the Global Control Bar (lines 170-184). (4) The non-functional 'Execute Simulation' button (lines 576-578) — no onClick handler exists, dead UI. (5) Any `ppiHistory` calculations or `calculateMaxLoan` calls that depend on monthlyBudget/mortgageTerm — these personal affordability metrics belong on AffordabilitySettings. Keep: (a) LTV band selector (activeLTV) — this is a chart display filter for which market rate to show, not a personal setting. (b) Series visibility toggles (visibleSeries). Keep the Rate Corridor chart, PPI chart, and LTV Arbitrage Matrix — these display market data only. The charts should display the current live rate without any affordability personalisation. Update the page subtitle from 'Purchasing Power Simulation' to 'Market Rate Intelligence' or similar. Update the PPI chart label that references monthlyBudget ('Estimated Loan Ceiling @ £X/mo') to show the current live 5yr fixed rate benchmark without personal budget inputs.

Runtime error in useMacroData.ts:35 — `raw.hpi_forecasts.map is not a function`. The normalization code assumes `hpi_forecasts` is an array but it is not in macro_trend.json, causing a TypeError that cascades to all components using `useMacroData()`. Fix: add `Array.isArray()` guard before calling .map() on hpi_forecasts in useMacroData.ts, or fix the data type in macro_trend.json.
notes: Confirmed via Playwright console error capture in AlphaBadge console-error test.
Error: `raw.hpi_forecasts.map is not a function` at useMacroData.ts:35.
This TypeError prevents useMacroData() from returning valid data.
MarketPulse returns null because data is null (due to this error).
Fix option A: add `Array.isArray(raw.hpi_forecasts) ? raw.hpi_forecasts.map(...) : []` in normalization.
Fix option B: ensure hpi_forecasts is an array in macro_trend.json (Data Analyst may need to fix data).

tasks.title: Systemic API failure crashes entire pages in test environment — LandingPage, Dashboard, PropertyDetail all show 'Something went wrong' errors. Root cause: useFinancialData.ts:14 throws unhandled 'TypeError: Failed to fetch' which crashes the page shell. Also useMacroData.ts:35 throws 'raw.hpi_forecasts.map is not a function' (QA-164). Fix: wrap API calls in try/catch with graceful fallback UI in both hooks. Pages must render content independently of API data availability — do not crash the page shell if financial/macro data is missing.
notes: Confirmed: LandingPage smoke test shows 'Something went wrong' for Market Situation Room (useFinancialData failure).
Confirmed: Dashboard smoke test shows 'Something went wrong' for properties table (API failure).
Confirmed: PropertyDetail smoke test fails on property load (API failure).
useFinancialData.ts:14 — unhandled 'Failed to fetch' crashes page shell.
These failures cascade and break smoke.spec.ts tests that were previously passing.

2026-04-01: Additional root cause found — PropertyContext.tsx line 39 uses hardcoded `http://localhost:3001/api` instead of relative `/api` path. This bypasses the Vite proxy and fails in Playwright sandbox. Fix: change PropertyContext.tsx line 39 from `http://localhost:3001/api` to `/api`.

tasks.title: PropertyContext.tsx uses hardcoded `http://localhost:3001/api` instead of relative `/api` — fails in Playwright sandbox (localhost blocked from localhost). Root cause of 'Property loading error: Failed to fetch'. Fix: change line 39 from `'http://localhost:3001/api'` to `'/api'` to match useFinancialData.ts.
notes: Confirmed via smoke test console errors.
PropertyContext.tsx line 39: `const API_BASE = IS_DEMO ? '/data' : 'http://localhost:3001/api'`
useFinancialData.ts line 8 (correct): `const API_BASE = IS_DEMO ? '/data' : '/api'`
Playwright sandbox blocks direct localhost:3001 requests.
Vite proxy forwards /api → localhost:3001 — only works with relative paths.

tasks.title: Data remediation: 3 Share of Freehold properties incorrectly have lease_years_remaining=0.0 instead of 999.0. The 0.0 values are likely missing-data placeholders that were never corrected.
alpha_score calculation (40% tenure quality) may be affected for these properties.

notes: Found via AUDIT_2026_03_30 follow-up (DAT-162, never formally created).
DAT-162 intent was to verify 999.0 as Share of Freehold sentinel.
Evidence: 15/16 Share of Freehold records already use 999.0 — strong convention established.
Share of Freehold = no lease = perpetual ownership = 999.0 is correct.

tasks.title: UI: MetroOverlay lines in Dashboard map are too faint. Per MAP_USABILITY_GUIDE.md, metro lines should use weight: 4.5, opacity: 0.75. Current values in Dashboard.tsx (line ~142): weight: 2.5, opacity: 0.6. This makes the metro overlay underemphasized against the dark Carto tiles. notes: CSS remediation in index.css (filter: none) IS applied.
MetroOverlay GeoJSON style in Dashboard.tsx is below spec.
MAP_USABILITY_GUIDE.md section B specifies: weight: 4.5, opacity: 0.75, lineJoin: round.

tasks.title: Runtime React error in Inbox.tsx — duplicate key warning: `leasehold_verification_required.json` appears twice in the listings array causing React to crash on render. Root cause: the inbox data source has a duplicate entry for `leasehold_verification_required.json`. Fix: deduplicate the inbox listings array before rendering by filename, OR fix the data source to not include duplicates.
notes: Confirmed via browser console: 'Encountered two children with the same key, leasehold_verification_required.json' at Inbox.tsx:320.
The `listings` array passed to `listings.map()` contains duplicate filename values.
Fix option A (Data Engineer): deduplicate listings array before render: `[...new Map(listings.map(l => [l.filename, l])).values()]`.
Fix option B (Data Engineer): fix the inbox data source to not include duplicate filenames.
QA-171 is the correct owner — Data Engineer owns data pipeline issues.

tasks.title: Runtime React error in MarketConditionsBar.tsx:141 — 'Objects are not valid as a React child (found: object with keys {value, source, source_url, methodology, last_refreshed})'. Root cause: `data.sdlt_countdown` is a provenance-wrapped object but is being rendered directly as a React child. The `sdlt_countdown` field in `macro_trend.json` has the provenance schema `{value, source, source_url, methodology, last_refreshed}` but `MarketConditionsBar` renders it as a plain string. Fix: use `extractValue(data.sdlt_countdown)` or `data.sdlt_countdown?.value` before rendering.
notes: Confirmed via browser console: 'Objects are not valid as a React child' at MarketConditionsBar.tsx:141.
The provenance schema wraps values in objects — e.g. `sdlt_countdown: {value: '12 weeks', source: '...'}`.
MarketConditionsBar renders `{data.sdlt_countdown}` directly as text, which fails when it's an object.
Fix: use `extractValue(data.sdlt_countdown)` or `data.sdlt_countdown?.value ?? data.sdlt_countdown` before rendering.
All provenance-wrapped fields in the macro schema should be checked for this issue.
